unresolved: Overload ambiguity: JsonDeserializer declares two overloads — the 2-arg deserialize(JsonParser, DeserializationContext) (abstract, primary) and the 3-arg deserialize(JsonParser, DeserializationContext, T intoValue). I assumed the issue refers to the 2-arg method. If the 3-arg signature is what changes (or both), a different/additional set applies: 3-arg overrides exist in JsonDeserializer, StdDelegatingDeserializer, DelegatingDeserializer, TypeWrappedDeserializer, CollectionDeserializer, MapDeserializer, AbstractDeserializer, BeanDeserializerBase/BeanDeserializer, ObjectArrayDeserializer, StringArrayDeserializer, EnumSetDeserializer, ReferenceTypeDeserializer, etc., plus 3-arg call sites (e.g. SettableBeanProperty.deserializeWith, InnerClassProperty/SetterlessProperty.deserializeAndSet, DefaultDeserializationContext, UntypedObjectDeserializer 3-arg branches, MapDeserializer/ReferenceTypeDeserializer update paths). These were deliberately excluded.

The JsonDeserializer 3-arg deserialize default body (JsonDeserializer.java:137) calls the 2-arg deserialize(p, ctxt); it is the same symbol name 'JsonDeserializer.deserialize' already listed, so it is covered, but note both overloads share that file:symbol key.
Anonymous-class overrides in tests (TestCustomEnumKeyDeserializer L218/L246, BeanDeserializerTest L244/257/272/287, CollectionDeserTest L360) have no stable name; labeled with file+line placeholders.

Test-directory override enumeration was produced by pattern search over src/test and could miss deserializers whose declaration spans lines unusually or uses unconventional formatting; not exhaustively call-graph-verified.

Dynamic dispatch / reflection: deserializers are also instantiated and invoked reflectively (HandlerInstantiator, module registration) and JsonDeserializer is a public extension point, so external/generated subclasses outside this repo would also need to change — not enumerable here.
